channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: India's Growth Under Pressure: Geopolitics Drives Up Costs, Fuels Inflation
item.description: India is navigating geopolitical risks by diversifying energy imports and supporting exporters. However, these shifts mean higher costs, fueling inflation and pressuring the rupee. While economic growth remains strong, these hidden expenses could pose future challenges.

item.title: Koiya International Plans Share Issue to Address Funding Needs
item.description: Koiya International, formerly Popees Cares, will hold a board meeting on March 20, 2026, to discuss its funding needs. The company plans to consider raising capital via a preferential share issue and other equity instruments to strengthen its finances, addressing past operational and financial difficulties.

item.title: Ethereum Foundation Sells 5,000 ETH to Bitmine, Boosting Crypto Treasury
item.description: The Ethereum Foundation sold 5,000 ETH to Bitmine Immersion Technologies for $10.2 million to fund core operations. This deal shows Bitmine's strong expansion as a leading ETH treasury, now holding over 4.5 million ETH. It also reflects institutional investors' continued interest in digital assets, despite widespread market caution and economic pressures.

item.title: Boris Johnson Calls Bitcoin 'Ponzi', Saylor Defends; Strategy Inc. Stock Faces Scrutiny
item.description: Former UK Prime Minister Boris Johnson has reignited debate by calling Bitcoin a "Ponzi scheme," drawing a sharp rebuke from Michael Saylor and MicroStrategy (Strategy Inc.). While Saylor champions Bitcoin's decentralized nature, the digital asset faces challenges. Gold has outperformed Bitcoin in 2025-2026, benefiting from broader economic uncertainty. This narrative casts a shadow over Strategy Inc., whose stock performance is heavily tied to Bitcoin's volatility, despite a strong analyst consensus.

item.title: Flipkart's ₹1 Milk Offer Triggers Farmer Outrage, Regulatory Probe
item.description: Bangalore Milk Union Ltd (BAMUL) president D K Suresh filed a complaint with the Competition Commission of India (CCI) against Flipkart. He accuses the e-commerce giant of selling milk for Re 1 per litre through deep discounts funded by investors. Suresh argues this predatory pricing hurts dairy farmers, whose milk procurement costs are ₹38-42 per litre, and threatens the cooperative dairy movement. Flipkart says these are limited seller promotions not affecting farmer payments, but the complaint highlights a clash between aggressive e-commerce growth and stable agricultural supply chains.

item.title: Gold Surges Past Expensive Indian Stocks
item.description: Gold has significantly outperformed Indian stocks for over a decade, a trend accelerating recently. Gold returned 82.86% in the year ending March 13, 2026, beating 97% of stocks. With Indian equities trading at high valuations, gold is increasingly vital for preserving purchasing power.

item.title: Tamil Nadu Invests ₹25Cr in Agnikul Cosmos, Fueling India's Space Race
item.description: Tamil Nadu's TIDCO has committed ₹25 crore to Agnikul Cosmos, a Chennai-based space technology firm. This funding, channeled through the TIDCO Startup Investment Policy 2025, aims to bolster Agnikul's operational readiness, scale its aerospace manufacturing, and advance its stage recovery program. The investment highlights the state's strategic push to cultivate deep-tech innovation and positions Agnikul as a key player in India's rapidly growing private space ecosystem.

item.title: Patanjali Foods Acquires ₹750 Crore Biscuit Plant to Boost Output
item.description: Patanjali Foods is acquiring a biscuit manufacturing plant and land rights for ₹750 crore from promoter group company Patanjali Ayurved Limited (PAL). This strategic move aims to boost its manufacturing capacity and efficiency, supporting the company's rapid expansion in the food sector. Approvals from YEIDA are pending.

item.title: Orchid Pharma Launches ₹600 Cr Plant to Cut Drug Import Reliance
item.description: Orchid Pharma is launching a major ₹600 crore pharmaceutical manufacturing plant in Kathua, Jammu. Supported by government PLI funds, the facility will produce Amino Cephalosporanic Acid (ACA), a key ingredient for antibiotics. This initiative is set to sharply reduce India's reliance on China for ACA, strengthening national health security and supply chains. The project also promises significant job creation in the region, supporting Prime Minister Modi's self-reliance vision.

item.title: Ferrari Launches Hybrid in India: UHNWIs and Trade Deal Fuel Luxury Boom
item.description: Ferrari's ₹10.37-crore Testarossa hybrid launch in Mumbai signals a major push into India's expanding ultra-luxury car market. Growth is fueled by a projected 50% rise in Ultra-High-Net-Worth Individuals by 2028 and the India-EU Free Trade Agreement, which will lower import duties on European cars. While rivals like Lamborghini, Porsche, and Aston Martin strengthen their Indian presence, Ferrari's hybrid focus aims to leverage global trends and rising domestic wealth.

item.title: Olympic Cards Defaults on ₹0.15 Cr HDFC Loan as Financial Strain Mounts
item.description: Olympic Cards Limited has reported a default on a ₹0.1517 crore term loan repayment to HDFC Bank Ltd., due February 20, 2026. This adds to the company's severe financial strain, marked by substantial outstanding borrowings and previous payment defaults. Investors await the bank's actions and the company's plan to manage its difficult financial situation.

item.title: Dev Labtech Ventures Invests AED 4.9 Lakh in UAE for Jewellery Trading
item.description: Dev Labtech Ventures Limited is expanding its global reach by investing AED 4,90,000 in its newly formed, wholly-owned subsidiary, DEV LABTECH TRADING FZCO, in the UAE. This strategic move aims to capitalize on the UAE's market opportunities and diversify the company's business into the trading of Jewellery, Diamonds, Pearls & Precious Stones. The investment marks a significant step towards building an international presence.

item.title: Indian Investors Choose Stocks Over Fixed Deposits for Long-Term Gains
item.description: Executives from Federal Bank and Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC report Indian investors are staying in equities and mutual funds, not opting for fixed deposits. This loyalty stems from consistent long-term mutual fund returns and investors prioritizing wealth building over market volatility.

item.title: Birla Award: Aditya Birla Group's Global Expansion Faces Tough Competition
item.description: Kumar Mangalam Birla received the Entrepreneur of the Decade award, acknowledging Aditya Birla Group's aggressive global expansion across cement, aluminium, and paints. The conglomerate has invested billions in overseas ventures, notably a large aluminium plant in Alabama, and is rapidly gaining market share in paints, challenging incumbents. This rapid growth, however, is occurring in highly competitive markets, with high valuations for key subsidiaries suggesting elevated investor expectations and potential risks if execution falters.

item.title: BAMUL Complains to CCI Over Flipkart's Aggressive Milk Pricing
item.description: Bangalore-based BAMUL has urged the Competition Commission of India (CCI) to investigate Flipkart's aggressive milk discounts, alleging the e-commerce giant is using milk as a 'loss-leader' below economic cost. This practice threatens the livelihoods of millions of small dairy farmers and distorts the entire dairy value chain. The complaint highlights concerns under Section 4 of the Competition Act, 2002, regarding abuse of dominant position. The move signals growing regulatory friction between investor-backed digital platforms and traditional agricultural sectors.

item.title: India Boosts Industry Self-Sufficiency with New Captive Power Rules
item.description: The Ministry of Power has enacted sweeping amendments to India's electricity norms for captive power generation, effective March 14, 2026. These changes, finalized after extensive consultations, aim to dismantle regulatory ambiguities, streamline business operations for industries, and align the captive power framework with national energy transition objectives. Key provisions simplify ownership structures, facilitate group captive arrangements, and offer protection against premature charges from distribution licensees, thereby enhancing industrial competitiveness and promoting decentralized energy generation.

item.title: India Exporters Face Soaring Costs, Delays Amid Red Sea Crisis
item.description: India's Commerce Minister announced new support for exporters, including cargo insurance, to combat trade disruptions caused by global tensions. While the government reassures on economic strength and expects the rupee to recover, exporters are grappling with soaring freight costs, longer shipping times, and potential losses. The country is also diversifying energy supplies and pursuing trade deals, but faces immediate financial pressures and a global economic slowdown.

item.title: India's ₹20K Cr Coal Gasification Project Amid Green Energy Shift
item.description: New Era Cleantech Solutions has launched India's first greenfield integrated coal gasification and carbon capture complex in Maharashtra, a ₹20,000 crore venture. The project aims to convert domestic coal into industrial chemicals, aligning with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative and addressing energy security concerns. However, it navigates a complex energy landscape marked by rapid renewable growth, the economic challenges of carbon capture technology, and the inherent risks of long-term coal dependency.

item.title: India Boosts Coal Output, Critical Minerals Amid Global Uncertainty
item.description: India's coal sector is ready for rising summer demand, says Union Minister G Kishan Reddy. The government is boosting domestic coal output and launching a National Critical Minerals Mission with ₹32,000 crore in incentives. This aims to secure vital resources and energy independence amid global geopolitical instability.

item.title: US Oil Waiver for India Draws Iran's Scathing Criticism
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister has sharply criticized the U.S. for issuing a 30-day waiver that allows India to continue importing crude oil from Russia. This U.S. policy shift comes after months of pressuring India to stop these purchases. Tehran called the move "pathetic," highlighting how global powers must balance energy security needs with sanctions and conflicts.

item.title: India Diversifies Oil Imports for Energy Stability Amid Global Turmoil
item.description: India's fuel supplies are stable amid global conflicts, according to Minister Piyush Goyal. The government is diversifying crude oil imports beyond the Middle East to countries like Canada, the US, and Russia to boost supply security. Kerosene supply has also increased as a precaution. Trade talks with the US and expansion of FTAs are progressing. Reserves are substantial, offering a buffer against disruptions, and India is engaging exporters on supply chain issues.

item.title: Maruti Interior Products Rights Issue: Odd Lot Rules Create Trading Hurdles
item.description: Maruti Interior Products Ltd has detailed the trading rules for its upcoming rights issue, emphasizing the market lot of 1,000 shares. Shareholders holding holdings not in multiples of 1,000 may face issues trading their shares or Rights Entitlements (REs), creating a trading hurdle. The company aims to raise up to ₹45.30 crore through this issue.

item.title: Brazil Faces Stablecoin Tax Fight, Hub Status at Risk
item.description: Brazil's crypto industry is mobilizing against a proposed financial transaction tax (IOF) on stablecoins, arguing it violates constitutional law and the 2022 Virtual Assets Law by misclassifying digital assets as foreign currency. This move threatens to undermine Brazil's position as a regulated crypto hub, despite significant market growth and adoption. Industry groups warn of legal disputes and a chilling effect on innovation.

item.title: India JV Aims for Global Isotope Supply: CIL & Chemtatva Invest ₹300 Cr
item.description: Cambridge Isotope Laboratories (CIL) and Chemtatva Chiral Solutions are forming a ₹300 crore joint venture in Telangana's Genome Valley to establish advanced manufacturing for stable isotope-labeled compounds. The initiative aims to create over 250 jobs and significantly reduce India's reliance on imports for these critical materials used in pharmaceuticals, diagnostics, and advanced electronics. This strategic move positions India to bolster its domestic capabilities and potentially challenge established global players in a high-value, specialized chemical segment.

item.title: Elpro International: GST Search Concludes With No Material Impact Reported
item.description: Elpro International Ltd informed exchanges that Central GST authorities conducted searches at its locations on March 13, 2026. The company stated the proceedings concluded the same day, with Elpro providing full cooperation. No material impact on financials or operations is currently reported.

item.title: Krishival Foods Promoter Buys ₹12.80 Lakh Shares, Ups Stake to 34.49%
item.description: Aparna Sujit Bangar, Promoter and Managing Director of Krishival Foods, has acquired 4,000 shares worth ₹12.80 lakh. This transaction, representing 0.02% of the total paid-up share capital, nudges her direct holding to 34.49%. The move signals management confidence amid the company's expansion plans.

item.title: Tamil Nadu's TIDCO Rebrands to Venture Catalyst, Funds Deep Tech with ₹50 Cr
item.description: Tamil Nadu Industrial Development Corporation (TIDCO) has initiated its Startup Investment Policy 2025 with ₹50 crore invested in space-tech firm Agnikul Cosmos and electric mobility startup Raptee Energy, ₹25 crore each. This marks TIDCO's rebranding to 'Venture Catalyst,' signaling a strategic pivot towards directly supporting deep-tech and sunrise sectors. These initial deployments underscore the state's ambition to foster innovation and secure leadership in emerging technologies, aiming to replicate past industrial successes with new-age companies.

item.title: US Hits Iran's Kharg Island Oil Hub, Spares Infrastructure
item.description: The US struck military targets on Iran's Kharg Island, a critical hub for nearly all its oil exports. The action deliberately avoided oil infrastructure to pressure Tehran while trying to prevent immediate global price shocks. Oil markets are watching closely for further disruption and volatility.

item.title: Avenue Supermarts Names Key Officials for Stock Disclosures from April 1, 2026
item.description: Avenue Supermarts has informed stock exchanges that specific key officials will now handle material information disclosures starting April 1, 2026. This step aims to streamline communication and ensure compliance with SEBI regulations.

item.title: SBI Flags RCOM Unit's Loan as Fraud, Eyes RBI Reporting
item.description: State Bank of India has classified Reliance Telecom Limited's (RTL) ₹125 crore loan as fraud, and will report it to the Reserve Bank of India. This comes as RTL and its parent, Reliance Communications (RCOM), are in corporate insolvency proceedings with resolution plans awaiting NCLT approval, adding a major regulatory challenge.

item.title: India Real Estate: Q1 Leasing Surge Met by AI Risks, Mideast Tensions
item.description: India's commercial property market started 2024 strong with higher leasing than last year. But experts warn that growing AI use and Mideast instability could slow future growth. While Tier-II cities and hotels look promising, office and luxury homes face new challenges, signaling a split market where traditional assets struggle but alternatives gain ground.

item.title: India's Property Market Thrives Amid AI Uncertainty
item.description: India's property market showed strong resilience in early 2026. Office leasing hit historic highs in 2025, driven by global tech centers, with low vacancies and rising rents. The housing market also grew, powered by premium buyers and favorable economic conditions. However, rapid AI adoption creates uncertainty about future office needs and job roles, even as AI promises to boost efficiency.

item.title: MSTR's Bitcoin Holdings Face Debt Pressure and Price Swings
item.description: MicroStrategy has significantly expanded its Bitcoin treasury, now holding over 738,731 BTC. The company recently acquired 17,994 BTC for $1.28 billion, funded by stock issuances. Despite this aggressive accumulation, its cumulative average purchase price of $75,862 per Bitcoin exceeds current market values, and its substantial debt load of over $8.2 billion introduces significant financial leverage and risk. While the stock has shown recent resilience, MSTR's valuation is increasingly tethered to Bitcoin's volatile price action.

item.title: DMart Names Kalpana Unadkat New Chairperson, Bhave Exits
item.description: Avenue Supermarts, which runs the DMart retail chain, has named Kalpana Unadkat as its new Chairperson, starting April 1, 2026. She will replace Chandrashekhar Bhave, who steps down March 31, 2026. Unadkat brings strong expertise in corporate law and governance.

item.title: Thomas Cook India Unlocks 49,196 ESOP Shares for Employees
item.description: Thomas Cook (India) Limited's Nomination and Remuneration Committee has authorized the transfer of 49,196 equity shares under its Employee Stock Option (ESOP) plans. This formalizes the distribution of company stock to employees who have exercised their options, managed through the ESOP Trust. The action clears the way for these shares to be allotted, affecting the company's total outstanding equity.

item.title: NIS Management Subsidiaries' Auditor Resigns; New Firm Search Begins
item.description: NIS Management Limited announced that M/s. Datta Roy & Associates, the statutory auditor for its five subsidiary companies, has resigned effective March 13, 2026. The firm cited professional commitments, stating no other material issues. The company must now appoint new auditors for these subsidiaries.

item.title: Satani Bearings Reclassifies Promoters to Public Shareholders
item.description: Satani Bearings Limited has completed its Open Offer, leading to a significant shift in its ownership. Key promoters are now reclassified as public shareholders as of March 13, 2026, in line with SEBI regulations. This change moves the company towards broader public shareholding.

item.title: Avenue Supermarts Vests 1.5M Stock Options at ₹299; New Shares Possible
item.description: Avenue Supermarts' ESOP Committee has approved the vesting of 15,12,909 stock options for eligible employees. These options, granted in March 2017, can be exercised at ₹299 each until June 13, 2026. While this incentivizes employees, it also presents a potential for share dilution for existing shareholders.

item.title: Fabtech Technologies Pushes Advantek Acquisition Deadline to March 31, 2026
item.description: Fabtech Technologies Cleanrooms Limited has pushed the completion deadline for its acquisition of Advantek Air Systems Private Limited to March 31, 2026. This extension is to finalize necessary documentation and execution formalities. The original acquisition intimation was made on February 12, 2026. The company will continue to update the stock exchange on further developments.

item.title: K&R Rail Engineering CFO Steps Down After Accident Injuries
item.description: K&R Rail Engineering Limited announced Chief Financial Officer Mr. Kulkarni Prahallada Rao will resign on March 5, 2026. His departure stems from injuries sustained in a recent motor vehicle accident. The company, already grappling with financial difficulties and a falling stock price, thanked Mr. Rao for his service.
